volumeTitle: The Woman of Andros. The Self-Tormentor. The Eunuch
label: The Woman of Andros (Section 2.5.425b - Section 3.1.480)

sections.label: Section 2.5.425b
sections.original: Nullane in re esse homini cuiquam fidem ?
sections.translation: Is there , in no case , putting trust in any man ? That is a true proverb which is wont to be commonly quoted , that all had rather it to be well for themselves than for another . I remember noticing , when I saw her , that she was a young woman of handsome figure ; wherefore I am the more disposed to excuse Pamphilus ,

sections.label: Section 2.5.430
sections.original: Si se illam in somnis quam illum amplecti maluit .
sections.translation: if he has preferred that he himself , rather than the other , should embrace her in his slumbers . I ’ ll carry back these tidings , that , in return for this evil he may inflict evil upon me . [*]

sections.label: Section 2.6.432
sections.original: Hic nunc me credit aliquam sibi fallaciam
sections.translation: He now supposes that I ’ m bringing some trick to bear against him , and that on that account I ’ ve remained here .

sections.label: Section 2.6.434
sections.translation: What does he say , Davus ? [*]

sections.label: Section 2.6.434b
sections.translation: Just as much as nothing . [*]

sections.label: Section 2.6.435
sections.translation: What , nothing ? Eh ?

sections.label: Section 2.6.435b
sections.translation: Nothing at all .

sections.label: Section 2.6.435c
sections.translation: And yet I certainly was expecting something .

sections.label: Section 2.6.436
sections.original: Praeter spem evenit : sentio : hoc male habet virum .
sections.translation: It has turned out contrary to your expectations . ( Aside .) I perceive it ; this vexes the man .

sections.label: Section 2.6.437
sections.translation: Are you able to tell me the truth ?

sections.label: Section 2.6.437b
sections.translation: I ? Nothing more easy .

sections.label: Section 2.6.438
sections.original: Num illi molestae quippiam hae sunt nuptiae
sections.translation: Is this marriage at all disagreeable to him , on account of his intimacy with this foreign woman ?

sections.label: Section 2.6.440
sections.original: Nihil hercle : aut , si adeo , bidui est aut tridui
sections.translation: No , faith ; or if at all , it is a two or three days ’ annoyance this — you understand . It will then cease . Moreover , he himself has thought over this matter in a proper way .

sections.label: Section 2.6.443
sections.translation: I commend him .

sections.label: Section 2.6.443b
sections.original: Dum licitum est ei , dumque aetas tulit ,
sections.translation: While it was allowed him , and while his years prompted him ,

sections.label: Section 2.6.444
sections.original: Amavit : tum id clam : cavit ne umquam infamiae
sections.translation: he intrigued ; even then it was secretly . He took precaution that that circumstance should never be a cause of disgrace to him , as behooves a man of principle ;

sections.label: Section 2.6.446
sections.original: Nunc uxore opus est : animum ad uxorem appulit .
sections.translation: now that he must have a wife , he has set his mind upon a wife .

sections.label: Section 2.6.447
sections.original: Subtristis visus est esse aliquantulum mihi .
sections.translation: He seemed to me to be somewhat melancholy in a slight degree .

sections.label: Section 2.6.448
sections.original: Nil propter hanc rem : sed est quod succenset tibi .
sections.translation: Not at all on account of her , but there ’ s something he blames you for .

sections.label: Section 2.6.449
sections.translation: What is it , pray ?

sections.label: Section 2.6.449b
sections.translation: It ’ s a childish thing .

sections.label: Section 2.6.449c
sections.translation: What is it ?

sections.label: Section 2.6.449d
sections.translation: Nothing at all .

sections.label: Section 2.6.449e
sections.translation: Nay but , tell me what it is .

sections.label: Section 2.6.450
sections.translation: He says that you are making too sparing preparations .

sections.label: Section 2.6.450b
sections.translation: What , I ?

sections.label: Section 2.6.450c
sections.translation: You .— He says that there has hardly been fare provided to the amount of ten drachmae . [*] — Does he seem to be bestowing a wife on his son ? Which one now , in preference , of my companions shall I invite to the dinner ? And , it must be owned ,

sections.label: Section 2.6.455
sections.original: Tu quoque perparce nimium . Non laudo .
sections.translation: you really are providing too parsimoniously — I do not commend you .

sections.label: Section 2.6.455b
sections.translation: Hold your tongue .

sections.label: Section 2.6.456
sections.translation: I ’ ve touched him up .

sections.label: Section 2.6.456b
sections.original: Ego istaec recte ut fiant videro .
sections.translation: I ’ ll see that these things are properly done . ( DAVUS goes into the house .) What ’ s the meaning of this ? What does this old rogue mean ? But if there ’ s any knavery here , why , he ’ s sure to be the source of the mischief .

sections.label: Section 3.1.459
sections.original: Ita pol quidem res est ut dixti Lesbia :
sections.translation: Upon my faith , the fact is really as you mentioned , Lesbia ,

sections.label: Section 3.1.460
sections.original: Fidelem haud ferme mulieri invenias virum .
sections.translation: you can hardly find a man constant to a woman .

sections.label: Section 3.1.461
sections.translation: This maid-servant comes from the Andrian .

sections.label: Section 3.1.461b
sections.translation: What do you say ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.461c
sections.translation: It is so .

sections.label: Section 3.1.462
sections.translation: But this Pamphilus —

sections.label: Section 3.1.462b
sections.translation: What is she saying ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.462c
sections.translation: Has proved his constancy .

sections.label: Section 3.1.462d
sections.translation: Hah !

sections.label: Section 3.1.463
sections.original: Utinam aut hic surdus aut haec muta facta sit .
sections.translation: I wish that either he were deaf , or she struck dumb .

sections.label: Section 3.1.464
sections.translation: For the child she brings forth , he has ordered to be brought up .

sections.label: Section 3.1.464b
sections.translation: O Jupiter !

sections.label: Section 3.1.465
sections.original: Quid ego audio ? Actum est siquidem haec vera praedicat .
sections.translation: What do I hear ! It ’ s all over , if indeed this woman speaks the truth .

sections.label: Section 3.1.466
sections.translation: You mention a good disposition on the part of the young man .

sections.label: Section 3.1.466b
sections.translation: A most excellent one . But follow me in-doors , that you mayn ’ t keep her waiting .

sections.label: Section 3.1.467b
sections.translation: I ’ ll follow .

sections.label: Section 3.1.468
sections.original: Quod remedium nunc huic malo inveniam ?
sections.translation: What remedy now shall I find for this mishap ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.468b
sections.translation: What does this mean ? Is he so infatuated ? The child of a foreign woman ? Now I understand ; ah !

sections.label: Section 3.1.470
sections.translation: scarcely even at last , in my stupidity , have I found it out .

sections.label: Section 3.1.470b
sections.translation: What does he say he has found out ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.471
sections.original: Haec primum adfertur iam mihi ab hoc fallacia :
sections.translation: This piece of knavery is being now for the first time palmed upon me by this fellow ; they are pretending that she ’ s in labor , in order that they may alarm Chremes .

sections.label: Section 3.1.473
sections.original: Iuno Lucina fer opem , serva me , obsecro .
sections.translation: Juno Lucina , [*] grant me thine aid , save me , I do entreat thee !

sections.label: Section 3.1.474
sections.original: Hui tam cito ? ridiculum : postquam ante ostium
sections.translation: Whew ! so sudden ? What nonsense ! As soon as

sections.label: Section 3.1.475
sections.original: Me audivit stare approperat . Non sat commode
sections.translation: she has heard that I ’ m standing before the door , she makes all haste . These incidents , Davus , have not been quite happily adapted by you as to the points of time .

sections.label: Section 3.1.476b
sections.translation: By me ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.477
sections.translation: Are your scholars forgetful ? [*]

sections.label: Section 3.1.477b
sections.translation: I don ’ t know what you are talking about .

sections.label: Section 3.1.478
sections.original: Hicine me si imparatum in veris nuptiis
sections.translation: If he at the real marriage of my son had taken me off my guard , what sport he would have made of me .

sections.label: Section 3.1.480
sections.original: Nunc huius periclo fit , ego in portu navigo .
sections.translation: Now it is at his own risk ; I ’ m sailing in harbor .
